content:
# Depth estimation must run on original frames, not RAUNE-enhanced (2026-04-08)

## What
Changed the fused RAUNE+depth batch to run Depth Anything on the original input frames instead of the RAUNE-enhanced output.

## Why
RAUNE color correction (designed for underwater red recovery) distorts the image characteristics that Depth Anything was trained on. Running depth on enhanced frames produces artifacts — inaccurate depth zone boundaries that cause visible grading artifacts in the output video. Confirmed via E2E test on 4K 120fps DJI footage.

## How to apply
If depth estimation ever appears to produce noisy or incorrect results, check whether the depth model input has been preprocessed. Depth Anything V2 expects natural/unmodified images.
